{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Technical Assistant Engineer",
  "description": "Abelinis is a third-party resource management company that provides and manages workforce solutions for clients such as Lafarge, Guinness, Friesland, and others. We are responsible for end-to-end resource management, including recruitment, payroll administration, and workforce/community engagement on behalf of these partner organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Technical Assistant Engineeer

Job Location: Akwa Ibom and Bayelsa

Job Summary


	The Technical Assistant Engineer will support business operations by providing on-site technical services at building and construction sites while generating qualified leads to support sales execution.
	The role requires strong field engagement, basic technical knowledge, and effective collaboration with the Field Sales team to drive timely business fulfilment.


Key Responsibilities


	Visit construction and building sites within assigned locations to provide required technical support and services.
	Identify, generate, and document potential business leads during site visits.
	Handover qualified leads to Field Sales Agents for fulfilment and closure.
	Ensure all generated leads are valid, required by clients, and capable of being fulfilled within seventy-two (72) hours.
	Maintain accurate records of site visits, leads generated, and activities carried out.
	Support the Field Sales Manager in executing field-related technical and operational tasks.
	Carry out any other duties assigned by management in line with business needs.


Requirements


	Minimum of an HND or Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Civil Engineering, Architecture, Building Technology, Construction Management, or any construction-related discipline.
	Basic understanding of construction processes, building materials, and site operations.
	Previous field, site, or technical support experience is an added advantage.
	Ability to identify business opportunities and engage clients professionally on-site.
	Strong communication, reporting, and interpersonal skills.
	Physically fit and willing to work extensively in the field.
	Willingness to travel and work across different locations within Nigeria as required.
	High level of integrity, reliability, and adaptability to changing work schedules.


Key Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Basic technical and site-handling knowledge
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Ability to identify business opportunities on-site
	Good reporting and documentation skills
	Willingness to work in the field and adapt to varying schedules.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@abelinis.ng using &quot;TAE BAYELSA/AKWAIBOM&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Applicants must reside in the location they are applying for.",

Technical Assistant Engineer Job at Abelinis




        
    

        
        Abelinis is a third-party resource management company that provides and manages workforce solutions for clients such as Lafarge, Guinness, Friesland, and others. We are responsible for end-to-end resource management, including recruitment, payroll administration, and workforce/community engagement on behalf of these partner organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Technical Assistant Engineeer

Job Location: Akwa Ibom and Bayelsa

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Technical Assistant Engineer will support business operations by providing on-site technical services at building and construction sites while generating qualified leads to support sales execution.
	The role requires strong field engagement, basic technical knowledge, and effective collaboration with the Field Sales team to drive timely business fulfilment.


Key Responsibilities


	Visit construction and building sites within assigned locations to provide required technical support and services.
	Identify, generate, and document potential business leads during site visits.
	Handover qualified leads to Field Sales Agents for fulfilment and closure.
	Ensure all generated leads are valid, required by clients, and capable of being fulfilled within seventy-two (72) hours.
	Maintain accurate records of site visits, leads generated, and activities carried out.
	Support the Field Sales Manager in executing field-related technical and operational tasks.
	Carry out any other duties assigned by management in line with business needs.


Requirements


	Minimum of an HND or Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering, Architecture, Building Technology, Construction Management, or any construction-related discipline.
	Basic understanding of construction processes, building materials, and site operations.
	Previous field, site, or technical support experience is an added advantage.
	Ability to identify business opportunities and engage clients professionally on-site.
	Strong communication, reporting, and interpersonal skills.
	Physically fit and willing to work extensively in the field.
	Willingness to travel and work across different locations within Nigeria as required.
	High level of integrity, reliability, and adaptability to changing work schedules.


Key Skills & Competencies:


	Basic technical and site-handling knowledge
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Ability to identify business opportunities on-site
	Good reporting and documentation skills
	Willingness to work in the field and adapt to varying schedules.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "TAE BAYELSA/AKWAIBOM" as the subject of the email.

Note: Applicants must reside in the location they are applying for.    

    

        Click here to Apply
